original_query: select s_name, s_address from supplier, nation where s_suppkey in ( select ps_suppkey from partsupp where ps_partkey in ( select p_partkey from part where p_name like 'burnished%' ) and ps_availqty > ( select 0.5 * sum(l_quantity) from lineitem where l_partkey = ps_partkey and l_suppkey = ps_suppkey and l_shipdate >= date '1996-01-01' and l_shipdate < date '1996-01-01' + interval '1' year ) ) and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'GERMANY' order by s_name;
rewritten_query:
WITH filtered_parts AS (
    SELECT p_partkey
    FROM part
    WHERE p_name LIKE 'burnished%'
),
part_aggregate AS (
    SELECT l_partkey, l_suppkey, 0.5 * SUM(l_quantity) AS half_sum
    FROM lineitem
    WHERE l_shipdate >= DATE '1996-01-01'
      AND l_shipdate < DATE '1997-01-01'
    GROUP BY l_partkey, l_suppkey
),
valid_suppliers AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ps.ps_suppkey
    FROM partsupp ps
    INNER JOIN filtered_parts fp ON ps.ps_partkey = fp.p_partkey
    INNER JOIN part_aggregate pa ON ps.ps_partkey = pa.l_partkey AND ps.ps_suppkey = pa.l_suppkey
    WHERE ps.ps_availqty > pa.half_sum
)
SELECT s.s_name, s.s_address
FROM supplier s
JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey
WHERE n.n_name = 'GERMANY'
  AND s.s_suppkey IN (SELECT ps_suppkey FROM valid_suppliers)
ORDER BY s.s_name;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replaced nested correlated subqueries with CTEs to precompute filtered parts and aggregates
Precomputed the aggregate for lineitem by (partkey, suppkey) to avoid repeated execution of the correlated subquery
Used explicit JOIN syntax instead of comma-separated tables in the FROM clause
Used DISTINCT in the valid_suppliers CTE to ensure unique supplier keys
